heading: ## Pre-registration Plan
body: We will preregister the analysis plan for Tests P1–P6 on OSF (placeholder: https://osf.io/TBD) prior to data collection, with planned date 2026-09-01.

heading: ## Sample Size Calculation
body: For detection of correlation r ≥ 0.6 at α = 0.05 and power 0.80, required N ≥ 19 paired observations (Fisher’s z-transformation). For FT1.1: N = 25 HSC samples per time point. For FT2.1: N = 30 paired divisions. For FT6.1: N = 20 animals per group. For FT6.2: N = 15 replicates per condition. All calculations assume two-sided test, α = 0.05, power = 0.80.

heading: ## Limitations
body:
1. Direct evidence for C1 and C2 in HSC is absent (P0 blocking problem).
2. In-sample R²(MCAI)=0.745 but LOO-CV mean=-0.093 indicates overfitting; ROS equation requires correction.
3. Power analysis for key experimental tests is placeholder-based (see Sample Size Calculation section).
4. Pre-registration plan is placeholder (osf.io/TBD) and not yet executed.
5. Some references remain unverified (marked with [PMID: TBD] or [DOI: TBD]).
6. The model assumes linear damage accumulation; non-linear feedback effects are not yet incorporated.
7. Consortium partnerships are preliminary and not formalized.

heading: ## Consortium / Partners
body:
- Yale University (Centriole biology, polyGlu imaging) — contact: TBD
- Stanford University (HSC lineage tracing, single-cell analysis) — contact: TBD
- Max Planck Institute for Biology of Ageing (C. elegans validation) — contact: TBD
- University of Cambridge (Computational modeling, MCOA integration) — contact: TBD
- LongevityCommon (Meta-theory coordination, data sharing platform) — contact: TBD

heading: ## Sample Size Calculation
body: For Peters-Hall replication experiment: N = 20 animals per group (control vs. treatment) to detect 30% difference in median lifespan with α = 0.05 and power 0.80. For CCP1 KO experiment: N = 10 animals per genotype. For LDC10 inhibition experiment: N = 15 replicates per condition. All calculations assume two-sided t-test, α = 0.05, power = 0.80. Effect sizes based on published literature (Peters 2020, Hall 2021).
